state.title: pkg/skills/loader.go
state.output:
<path>/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/skills/loader.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // Package skills provides skill discovery and loading from ~/.claude/skills/.
2: package skills
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"errors"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
11: )
12: 
13: var (
14: 	// ErrSkillNotFound is returned when a skill cannot be found.
15: 	ErrSkillNotFound = errors.New("skill not found")
16: 	// ErrNoFrontmatter is returned when skill content has no YAML frontmatter.
17: 	ErrNoFrontmatter = errors.New("no YAML frontmatter found")
18: )
19: 
20: // SkillMetadata represents parsed skill YAML frontmatter.
21: type SkillMetadata struct {
22: 	Name         string   `yaml:"name"`
23: 	SkillType    string   `yaml:"skill-type"`
24: 	Audience     string   `yaml:"audience"`
25: 	Spawnable    bool     `yaml:"spawnable"`
26: 	Composable   bool     `yaml:"composable"`
27: 	Category     string   `yaml:"category"`
28: 	Description  string   `yaml:"description"`
29: 	Dependencies []string `yaml:"dependencies"`
30: }
31: 
32: // Loader discovers and loads skills from a skills directory.
33: type Loader struct {
34: 	skillsDir string
35: }
36: 
37: // NewLoader creates a new skill loader for the given skills directory.
38: func NewLoader(skillsDir string) *Loader {
39: 	return &Loader{skillsDir: skillsDir}
40: }
41: 
42: // DefaultLoader creates a loader for the default ~/.claude/skills/ directory.
43: func DefaultLoader() *Loader {
44: 	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
45: 	if err != nil {
46: 		return &Loader{skillsDir: ""}
47: 	}
48: 	return &Loader{skillsDir: filepath.Join(home, ".claude", "skills")}
49: }
50: 
51: // FindSkillPath finds the path to a skill's SKILL.md file.
52: // It searches:
53: // 1. Direct symlinks: skillsDir/skillName -> .../SKILL.md
54: // 2. Subdirectories: skillsDir/*/skillName/SKILL.md
55: func (l *Loader) FindSkillPath(skillName string) (string, error) {
56: 	if l.skillsDir == "" {
57: 		return "", ErrSkillNotFound
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	// Check direct symlink first: skillsDir/skillName/SKILL.md
61: 	directPath := filepath.Join(l.skillsDir, skillName, "SKILL.md")
62: 	if _, err := os.Stat(directPath); err == nil {
63: 		return directPath, nil
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	// Search subdirectories: skillsDir/*/skillName/SKILL.md
67: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(l.skillsDir)
68: 	if err != nil {
69: 		return "", ErrSkillNotFound
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	for _, entry := range entries {
73: 		if !entry.IsDir() {
74: 			continue
75: 		}
76: 		// Skip hidden directories
77: 		if strings.HasPrefix(entry.Name(), ".") {
78: 			continue
79: 		}
80: 
81: 		potentialPath := filepath.Join(l.skillsDir, entry.Name(), skillName, "SKILL.md")
82: 		if _, err := os.Stat(potentialPath); err == nil {
83: 			return potentialPath, nil
84: 		}
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	return "", ErrSkillNotFound
88: }
89: 
90: // LoadSkillContent loads the full content of a skill's SKILL.md file.
91: func (l *Loader) LoadSkillContent(skillName string) (string, error) {
92: 	path, err := l.FindSkillPath(skillName)
93: 	if err != nil {
94: 		return "", err
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	content, err := os.ReadFile(path)
98: 	if err != nil {
99: 		return "", err
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	return string(content), nil
103: }
104: 
105: // LoadSkillWithDependencies loads a skill's content with all dependencies prepended.
106: // Dependencies are loaded in order and their content is prepended before the main skill.
107: // This provides composable skill inheritance where worker-base patterns are available
108: // to all skills that depend on it.
109: func (l *Loader) LoadSkillWithDependencies(skillName string) (string, error) {
110: 	// Load main skill content
111: 	mainContent, err := l.LoadSkillContent(skillName)
112: 	if err != nil {
113: 		return "", err
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	// Parse metadata to check for dependencies
117: 	metadata, err := ParseSkillMetadata(mainContent)
118: 	if err != nil {
119: 		// If we can't parse metadata, just return the main content
120: 		return mainContent, nil
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	// If no dependencies, return as-is
124: 	if len(metadata.Dependencies) == 0 {
125: 		return mainContent, nil
126: 	}
127: 
128: 	// Load each dependency and build combined content
129: 	var combined strings.Builder
130: 
131: 	for _, dep := range metadata.Dependencies {
132: 		depContent, err := l.LoadSkillContent(dep)
133: 		if err != nil {
134: 			// Dependency not found - could log a warning here, but continue
135: 			continue
136: 		}
137: 
138: 		// Strip the frontmatter from dependency content since the main skill
139: 		// already has its own frontmatter
140: 		depBody := stripFrontmatter(depContent)
141: 		combined.WriteString(depBody)
142: 		combined.WriteString("\n\n")
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	// Append main skill content (keeping its frontmatter)
146: 	combined.WriteString(mainContent)
147: 
148: 	return combined.String(), nil
149: }
150: 
151: // stripFrontmatter removes YAML frontmatter from skill content, returning just the body.
152: func stripFrontmatter(content string) string {
153: 	if !strings.HasPrefix(content, "---") {
154: 		return content
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	// Find the closing ---
158: 	endIndex := strings.Index(content[3:], "---")
159: 	if endIndex == -1 {
160: 		return content
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	// Return content after the closing ---, trimming leading newlines
164: 	afterFrontmatter := content[3+endIndex+3:]
165: 	return strings.TrimLeft(afterFrontmatter, "\n")
166: }
167: 
168: // ParseSkillMetadata extracts YAML frontmatter from skill content.
169: func ParseSkillMetadata(content string) (*SkillMetadata, error) {
170: 	// YAML frontmatter is delimited by ---
171: 	if !strings.HasPrefix(content, "---") {
172: 		return nil, ErrNoFrontmatter
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	// Find the closing ---
176: 	endIndex := strings.Index(content[3:], "---")
177: 	if endIndex == -1 {
178: 		return nil, ErrNoFrontmatter
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	// Extract frontmatter YAML (between the --- delimiters)
182: 	frontmatter := content[3 : 3+endIndex]
183: 
184: 	var metadata SkillMetadata
185: 	if err := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(frontmatter), &metadata); err != nil {
186: 		return nil, err
187: 	}
188: 
189: 	return &metadata, nil
190: }
191: 

(End of file - total 191 lines)
</content>

// runPreFlightChecks performs all pre-spawn validation checks.
// Returns usage check result for telemetry, or error if any check fails.
func runPreFlightChecks(input *SpawnInput, preCheckDir string) (*gates.RateLimitCheckResult, error) {
	// Check for --bypass-triage flag (required for manual spawns)
	// Daemon-driven spawns skip this check (issue already triaged)
	if err := gates.CheckTriageBypass(input.DaemonDriven, spawnBypassTriage, input.SkillName, input.Task); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	// Log the triage bypass for Phase 2 review (only for manual bypasses, not daemon-driven)
	if !input.DaemonDriven && spawnBypassTriage {
		gates.LogTriageBypass(input.SkillName, input.Task)
	}

	// Check concurrency limit before spawning
	if err := gates.CheckConcurrency(input.ServerURL, spawnMaxAgents, extractBeadsIDFromTitle); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	// Proactive rate limit monitoring: warn at 80%, block at 95%
	usageCheckResult, usageErr := gates.CheckRateLimit()
	if usageErr != nil {
		// usageErr contains formatted blocking message
		return nil, usageErr
	}

	// STRATEGIC-FIRST ORCHESTRATION: Check for hotspots in task target area
	// In hotspot areas (5+ bugs, persistent failures), strategic approach is recommended
	// Warning shown but spawn proceeds (non-blocking)
	gates.CheckHotspot(preCheckDir, input.Task, input.SkillName, input.DaemonDriven, func(dir, t string) (*gates.HotspotResult, error) {
		result, err := RunHotspotCheckForSpawn(dir, t)
		if err != nil || result == nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		return &gates.HotspotResult{HasHotspots: result.HasHotspots, Warning: result.Warning}, nil
	})

	return usageCheckResult, nil
}

411: // LoadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace loads skill content and generates workspace name.
412: // The ensureScaffoldingFunc is called to check/initialize scaffolding (passed from cmd/orch).
413: func LoadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace(skillName, projectName, task, projectDir string, autoInit, noTrack bool, ensureScaffoldingFunc func(string, bool, bool) error) (
414: 	skillContent, workspaceName string,
415: 	isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool,
416: 	err error) {
417: 
418: 	// Check and optionally auto-initialize scaffolding
419: 	if ensureScaffoldingFunc != nil {
420: 		if err := ensureScaffoldingFunc(projectDir, autoInit, noTrack); err != nil {
421: 			return "", "", false, false, err
422: 		}
423: 	}
424: 
425: 	// Load skill content with dependencies (e.g., worker-base patterns)
426: 	loader := skills.DefaultLoader()
427: 
428: 	// First load raw skill content (without dependencies) to detect skill type
429: 	// This is needed because LoadSkillWithDependencies puts dependency content first,
430: 	// which means the main skill's frontmatter isn't at the start of the combined content
431: 	rawSkillContent, err := loader.LoadSkillContent(skillName)
432: 	if err == nil {
433: 		if metadata, err := skills.ParseSkillMetadata(rawSkillContent); err == nil {
434: 			isOrchestrator = metadata.SkillType == "policy" || metadata.SkillType == "orchestrator"
435: 		}
436: 	}
437: 	// Detect meta-orchestrator by skill name (not skill-type)
438: 	// This enables tiered context templates for different orchestrator levels
439: 	if skillName == "meta-orchestrator" {
440: 		isMetaOrchestrator = true
441: 	}
442: 
443: 	// Generate workspace name
444: 	// Meta-orchestrators use "meta-" prefix instead of project prefix for visual distinction
445: 	// Orchestrators use "orch" skill prefix instead of "work" for visual distinction from workers
446: 	workspaceName = spawn.GenerateWorkspaceName(projectName, skillName, task, spawn.WorkspaceNameOptions{
447: 		IsMetaOrchestrator: isMetaOrchestrator,
448: 		IsOrchestrator:     isOrchestrator,
449: 	})
450: 
451: 	// Now load full skill content with dependencies for the actual spawn
452: 	skillContent, err = loader.LoadSkillWithDependencies(skillName)
453: 	if err != nil {
454: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: could not load skill '%s': %v\n", skillName, err)
455: 		skillContent = "" // Continue without skill content
456: 	}
457: 
458: 	return skillContent, workspaceName, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator, nil
459: }
460: 
461: // SetupBeadsTracking determines beads ID and manages issue lifecycle.
462: // Returns final beads ID (empty if untracked), or error if setup fails.
463: func SetupBeadsTracking(skillName, task, projectName, beadsIssueFlag string, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool, serverURL string, noTrack bool, createBeadsFn func(string, string, string) (string, error)) (string, error) {
464: 	// Determine beads ID - either from flag, create new issue, or skip if --no-track
465: 	// Orchestrators skip beads tracking entirely - they're interactive sessions with Dylan,
466: 	// not autonomous tasks. SESSION_HANDOFF.md is richer than beads comments.
467: 	skipBeadsForOrchestrator := isOrchestrator || isMetaOrchestrator
468: 	beadsID, err := determineBeadsID(projectName, skillName, task, beadsIssueFlag, noTrack || skipBeadsForOrchestrator, createBeadsFn)
469: 	if err != nil {
470: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to determine beads ID: %w", err)
471: 	}
472: 	if skipBeadsForOrchestrator {
473: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (orchestrator session)")
474: 	} else if noTrack {
475: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (--no-track)")
476: 	}
477: 
478: 	// Check for retry patterns on existing issues - surface to prevent blind respawning
479: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
480: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
481: 		if stats, err := verify.GetFixAttemptStats(beadsID); err == nil && stats.IsRetryPattern() {
482: 			warning := verify.FormatRetryWarning(stats)
483: 			if warning != "" {
484: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n%s\n", warning)
485: 			}
486: 		}
487: 	}
488: 
489: 	// Check if issue is already being worked on (prevent duplicate spawns)
490: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
491: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
492: 		if issue, err := verify.GetIssue(beadsID); err == nil {
493: 			if issue.Status == "closed" {
494: 				return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already closed", beadsID)
495: 			}
496: 			if issue.Status == "in_progress" {
497: 				// Check if there's a truly active agent for this issue
498: 				// OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so we must verify liveness not just existence
499: 				client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
500: 				sessions, _ := client.ListSessions("")
501: 				for _, s := range sessions {
502: 					if strings.Contains(s.Title, beadsID) {
503: 						// Session exists - but is it actually active (recently updated)?
504: 						// Use 30 minute threshold - if no activity, session is stale
505: 						if client.IsSessionActive(s.ID, 30*time.Minute) {
506: 							return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already in_progress with active agent (session %s). Use 'orch send %s' to interact or 'orch abandon %s' to restart", beadsID, s.ID, s.ID, beadsID)
507: 						}
508: 						// Session exists but is stale - log and continue (allow respawn)
509: 						fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Note: found stale session %s for issue %s (no activity in 30m)\n", s.ID[:12], beadsID)
510: 					}
511: 				}
512: 				// No active session - check if Phase: Complete was reported
513: 				// If so, orchestrator needs to run 'orch complete' before respawning
514: 				if complete, err := verify.IsPhaseComplete(beadsID); err == nil && complete {
515: 					return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s has Phase: Complete but is not closed. Run 'orch complete %s' first", beadsID, beadsID)
516: 				}
517: 				// In progress but no active agent and not Phase: Complete - warn but allow respawn
518: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: issue %s is in_progress but no active agent found. Respawning.\n", beadsID)
519: 			}

(File has more lines. Use 'offset' parameter to read beyond line 519)
</content>

part.text:
**Analyzing deploy path resolution**

I've clarified how deploy uses relative paths based on the source directory: if deploying from exactly inside a `.skillc` dir, the relative path is ".", making the target output dir the same as the target; if deploying from the parent of `.skillc`, the relative path still computes as ".", so output stays at the root. This confirms the behavior for `skillc deploy` source path handling.

**Clarifying deploy path effects and audit plans**

Deploy output paths depend on the source path: running deploy from the skill root flattens output to target/SKILL.md, while from source root with specific sourcePath preserves meta/orchestrator dirs. I'm planning to document this and map load paths for OpenCode and orch-go, noting three known output locations. I'll draft investigation and probe files capturing commands used, reading/code review done, and model impact, including verifying existence of verification specs and synthesizing final audit summaries.

590: // ExtractFirstParagraph extracts the first non-header, non-comment paragraph from markdown content.
591: // It skips headers (lines starting with #), HTML comments, and horizontal rules.
592: // Returns the first block of actual text content.
593: // This supports the "Progressive Disclosure" principle: auto-generating summaries when
594: // not explicitly provided, enabling agents to load abbreviated context first.
595: // See DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md for details.
596: func ExtractFirstParagraph(content string) string {
597: 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
598: 	var paragraphLines []string
599: 	inParagraph := false
600: 	inHTMLComment := false
601: 
602: 	for _, line := range lines {
603: 		trimmedLine := strings.TrimSpace(line)
604: 
605: 		// Handle HTML comments (can span multiple lines)
606: 		if strings.Contains(trimmedLine, "<!--") {
607: 			if !strings.Contains(trimmedLine, "-->") {
608: 				inHTMLComment = true
609: 			}
610: 			continue
611: 		}
612: 		if inHTMLComment {
613: 			if strings.Contains(trimmedLine, "-->") {
614: 				inHTMLComment = false
615: 			}
616: 			continue
617: 		}
618: 
619: 		// Skip empty lines before finding paragraph
620: 		if trimmedLine == "" {
621: 			if inParagraph {
622: 				// End of paragraph
623: 				break
624: 			}
625: 			continue
626: 		}
627: 
628: 		// Skip headers
629: 		if strings.HasPrefix(trimmedLine, "#") {
630: 			if inParagraph {
631: 				// Hit another header, end of paragraph
632: 				break
633: 			}
634: 			continue
635: 		}
636: 
637: 		// Skip horizontal rules
638: 		if trimmedLine == "---" || trimmedLine == "***" || trimmedLine == "___" {
639: 			if inParagraph {
640: 				// Hit a horizontal rule, end of paragraph
641: 				break
642: 			}
643: 			continue
644: 		}
645: 
646: 		// Found content
647: 		inParagraph = true
648: 		paragraphLines = append(paragraphLines, line)
649: 	}
650: 
651: 	return strings.Join(paragraphLines, "\n")
652: }
653: 
654: // templateMarkerRegex matches SKILL-TEMPLATE markers like:
655: // <!-- SKILL-TEMPLATE: name --><!-- /SKILL-TEMPLATE -->
656: // or with content between:
657: // <!-- SKILL-TEMPLATE: name -->old content<!-- /SKILL-TEMPLATE -->
658: var templateMarkerRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`<!--\s*SKILL-TEMPLATE:\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s*-->(.*?)<!--\s*/SKILL-TEMPLATE\s*-->`)
659: 
660: // expandTemplates replaces SKILL-TEMPLATE markers with content from template sources.
661: // This implements the "Template Expansion" principle: common patterns across skills
662: // should be shared through explicit template markers rather than copy-paste.
663: // See DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md for details.
664: func expandTemplates(content string, templates map[string]string, templateSources map[string]string) (string, error) {
665: 	var expandError error
666: 
667: 	result := templateMarkerRegex.ReplaceAllStringFunc(content, func(match string) string {
668: 		if expandError != nil {
669: 			return match // Don't process if we already have an error
670: 		}
671: 
672: 		// Extract template name from the match
673: 		submatches := templateMarkerRegex.FindStringSubmatch(match)
674: 		if len(submatches) < 2 {
675: 			return match
676: 		}
677: 		templateName := submatches[1]
678: 
679: 		// Check if template source exists
680: 		templatePath, exists := templateSources[templateName]
681: 		if !exists {
682: 			expandError = fmt.Errorf("unknown template marker '%s': no matching template_sources entry in skill.yaml", templateName)
683: 			return match
684: 		}
685: 
686: 		// Get the template content
687: 		templateContent, loaded := templates[templateName]
688: 		if !loaded {
689: 			expandError = fmt.Errorf("template '%s' (from %s) was not loaded", templateName, templatePath)
690: 			return match
691: 		}
692: 
693: 		// Build the replacement with preserved marker and auto-generated comment
694: 		var replacement strings.Builder
695: 		replacement.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("<!-- SKILL-TEMPLATE: %s -->\n", templateName))
696: 		replacement.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("<!-- Auto-generated from %s -->\n\n", templatePath))
697: 		replacement.WriteString(templateContent)
698: 		replacement.WriteString("\n\n<!-- /SKILL-TEMPLATE -->")
699: 
700: 		return replacement.String()
701: 	})
702: 
703: 	if expandError != nil {
704: 		return "", expandError
705: 	}
706: 
707: 	return result, nil
708: }
709: 
710: // generateHeader creates the self-describing header (for backwards compatibility).
711: // This implements the "Self-Describing Artifacts" principle: generated files must contain
712: // their own operating instructions so agents with no memory can modify them correctly.
713: // The header answers five essential questions: what is this, what NOT to do, where is
714: // source, how to modify, and when generated. See DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md for details.
715: func generateHeader() string {
716: 	return generateHeaderWithSource(".skillc/")
717: }
718: 
719: // generateDeployHeader creates a header specifically for deployed files with full source and target paths

40: ```bash
41: $ readlink ~/.claude/skills/orchestrator
42: policy/orchestrator
43: 
44: $ ls -la ~/.claude/skills/orchestrator
45: lrwxr-xr-x  1 dylanconlin  staff  19 Nov 30 14:15 /Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/orchestrator -> policy/orchestrator
46: ```
47: 
48: All other meta skills use `meta/` prefix in their symlinks:
49: ```
50: analyze-skill-usage -> meta/analyze-skill-usage
51: audit-claude-md -> meta/audit-claude-md  
52: testing-skills-with-subagents -> meta/testing-skills-with-subagents
53: writing-skills -> meta/writing-skills
54: orchestrator -> policy/orchestrator  # THE OUTLIER
55: ```
56: 
57: **Source:** `ls -la ~/.claude/skills/ | grep " -> " | sort -k11`
58: 
59: **Significance:** Orchestrator is the only skill using `policy/` prefix. This explains why `skillc deploy` doesn't update the skill being used - it deploys to `meta/orchestrator` but the symlink resolves to `policy/orchestrator`.
60: 
61: ---
62: 
63: ### Finding 2: Skillc deploys based on source directory structure, not skill-type
64: 
65: **Evidence:**
66: ```bash
67: $ cd ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src && ~/bin/skillc deploy --target ~/.claude/skills/
68: ✓ Deployed /Users/dylanconlin/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/.skillc to /Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md
69: ```
70: 
71: The source is at `meta/orchestrator/.skillc/` → deploys to `meta/orchestrator/`.
72: The `skill-type: policy` in skill.yaml has no effect on deployment path.
73: 
74: **Source:** `~/bin/skillc deploy --target ~/.claude/skills/` output, `~/Documents/personal/skillc/cmd/skillc/main.go:handleDeploy()`
75: 
76: **Significance:** The deploy logic in skillc preserves source directory structure via `filepath.Rel()` - it doesn't read skill-type to determine target path. This is correct behavior for the compiler, but the symlink setup was mistakenly created expecting skill-type-based paths.
77: 
78: ---
79: 
80: ### Finding 3: policy/ directory was created manually after meta/
81: 
82: **Evidence:**
83: ```bash
84: $ stat ~/.claude/skills/policy
85: Birth time: "Nov 30 14:15:58 2025"
86: 
87: $ stat ~/.claude/skills/meta  
88: Birth time: "Nov 26 14:50:43 2025"
89: ```
90: 
91: The policy directory was created 4 days after meta, and the orchestrator symlink was changed on Nov 30 to point to `policy/orchestrator`.
92: 
93: **Source:** `stat` command output on both directories
94: 
95: **Significance:** Someone (likely an agent) created `policy/` directory expecting skillc to deploy based on skill-type. This was a misunderstanding of how skillc works. The result: two copies of orchestrator skill exist, with manual workaround copying from meta to policy.
96: 
97: ---
98: 
99: ## Synthesis
100: 
101: **Key Insights:**
102: 
103: 1. **Symlink misconfiguration, not skillc bug** - Skillc correctly deploys based on source directory structure. The symlink was incorrectly changed to point to `policy/orchestrator` when the actual deploy target is `meta/orchestrator`.
104: 
105: 2. **Orphaned policy/ directory** - The policy directory contains stale copies and serves no purpose. It was created based on a misunderstanding that skill-type would determine deployment path.
106: 
107: 3. **Pattern: all skills use source directory prefix** - Every other skill's symlink follows the pattern `skill-name -> {source-dir}/skill-name`. Orchestrator should follow: `orchestrator -> meta/orchestrator`.
108: 
109: **Answer to Investigation Question:**
110: 
111: This is NOT a skillc bug - it's an incorrect symlink setup. Skillc correctly deploys `meta/orchestrator/.skillc/` to `~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md`. The symlink at `~/.claude/skills/orchestrator` was incorrectly changed on Nov 30 to point to `policy/orchestrator`, which is a manually-created directory that doesn't receive skillc updates.
112: 
113: **Fix:** Change symlink to point to `meta/orchestrator` and remove the orphaned `policy/` directory.
114: 
115: ---
116: 
117: ## Structured Uncertainty
118: 
119: **What's tested:**
120:

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: .kb/investigations/2026-02-18-inv-audit-full-skillc-pipeline.md
+---
+linked_issues:
+  - orch-go-1050
+---
+<!--
+D.E.K.N. Summary - 30-second handoff for fresh Claude
+Fill this at the END of your investigation, before marking Complete.
+-->
+
+## Summary (D.E.K.N.)
+
+**Delta:** Mapped the end-to-end skillc pipeline from source templates to deploy targets and the actual spawn-time load path for orchestrator skills, including all on-disk copies with timestamps.
+
+**Evidence:** skillc deploy preserves relative paths from the provided source root, so running deploy from a skill directory flattens output to `target/SKILL.md`; orch-go loads skills exclusively from `~/.claude/skills/**/SKILL.md` at spawn time, not from `~/.opencode/skill/`.
+
+**Knowledge:** The canonical orchestrator skill for orch-go is `~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (loaded by `pkg/skills/loader.go`). Multiple stale copies exist (`~/.claude/skills/src/**`, `~/.opencode/skill/**`, `~/Documents/personal/orch-cli/skills/**`) because deploy never cleans old directories and OpenCode scans multiple roots.
+
+**Next:** Align deploy workflow to always run from `~/orch-knowledge/skills/src` (or pass that path explicitly), and clean stale targets that can be discovered by OpenCode or humans.
+
+---
+
+# Investigation: Full Skillc Pipeline Audit
+
+**Question:** How does skillc compile and deploy orchestrator skills, where do copies land on disk, and which file is actually loaded at spawn time?
+
+**Started:** 2026-02-18
+**Updated:** 2026-02-18
+**Owner:** orch-go-1050
+**Phase:** Complete
+**Next Step:** None
+**Status:** Complete
+
+---
+
+## Pipeline Map (Source -> Compile -> Deploy -> Load)
+
+```
+~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/.skillc/
+  skill.yaml
+  SKILL.md.template
+  reference/
+        |
+        | skillc build (CompileWithStats)
+        v
+  ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md
+        |
+        | skillc deploy --target <target-dir> <source-root>
+        v
+  <target-dir>/<relPath>/SKILL.md
+        |
+        | orch spawn
+        v
+  ~/.claude/skills/**/SKILL.md (loaded at spawn time)
+```
+
+Key: `relPath` is computed from `absSourcePath` to the parent of `.skillc`. If you run deploy from inside a skill directory, `relPath` becomes `.`, so the output lands at `target/SKILL.md` (root) rather than `target/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md`.
+
+---
+
+## Findings
+
+### Finding 1: Source-of-truth lives in orch-knowledge `.skillc` directories
+
+**Evidence:** `~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/.skillc/` contains `skill.yaml` and `SKILL.md.template`. The manifest sets `output: SKILL.md` and `type: skill`, which triggers SKILL.md frontmatter emission.
+
+**Source:** `/Users/dylanconlin/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/.skillc/skill.yaml`
+
+**Significance:** This is the only authoritative place to edit orchestrator skill content. All other copies are generated artifacts and should be treated as deploy targets or stale caches.
+
+---
+
+### Finding 2: Compile step determines output filename from manifest output
+
+**Evidence:** `compiler.CompileWithOutput()` sets `outputPath` based on manifest `output` (default `CLAUDE.md`) and joins it with the base dir. The orchestrator manifest specifies `output: SKILL.md` so compilation emits `SKILL.md` in the parent directory of `.skillc`.
+
+**Source:** `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/skillc/pkg/compiler/compiler.go`
+
+**Significance:** This is why `SKILL.md` appears directly under `skills/src/meta/orchestrator/` after local builds.
+
+---
+
+### Finding 3: Deploy path preserves directory structure relative to the provided source root
+
+**Evidence:** `handleDeploy()` computes `relPath = filepath.Rel(absSourcePath, baseDir)` where `baseDir` is the parent of `.skillc`. The target output directory is `targetDir/relPath`. If `absSourcePath` is the skill directory (e.g., `.../skills/src/meta/orchestrator`), `relPath` is `.`, so deploy writes to `targetDir/SKILL.md`. If `absSourcePath` is the higher-level root (`.../skills/src`), `relPath` becomes `meta/orchestrator`, so deploy writes to `targetDir/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md`.
+
+**Source:** `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/skillc/cmd/skillc/main.go` (deploy logic)
+
+**Significance:** This explains the recurring mismatch where deploys land in `~/.opencode/skill/SKILL.md` instead of `~/.opencode/skill/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` when deploy is run from inside a skill directory.
+
+---
+
+### Finding 4: Deployed copies on disk (timestamps + staleness)
+
+**Evidence:** The following orchestrator skill files exist with the listed modification times:
+
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (Feb 18 14:13:33 2026) - Canonical orch-go load target
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/.config/opencode/agent/orchestrator.md` (Feb 18 14:13:33 2026) - Canonical OpenCode agent file
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/.opencode/skill/SKILL.md` (Feb 18 14:08:55 2026) - Flattened deploy output (depends on deploy root)
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/.opencode/skill/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (Feb 18 13:14:50 2026) - Older than canonical
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (Feb 18 09:18:39 2026) - Extra deploy target (stale copy)
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (Feb 18 09:17:28 2026) - Extra deploy target (stale copy)
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (Feb 11 10:53:31 2026) - Local compile artifact
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-cli/skills/orchestrator/SKILL.md` (Dec 14 13:18:20 2025) - Legacy repo copy
+
+**Source:** `stat -f "%Sm %N" ...` output
+
+**Significance:** Multiple stale copies persist because `skillc deploy` only writes new output; it never cleans old paths. These stale copies are discoverable by OpenCode skill scanners or humans, leading to accidental edits in the wrong file.
+
+---
+
+### Finding 5: Orch-go load path is fixed to ~/.claude/skills/
+
+**Evidence:** `skills.DefaultLoader()` hardcodes `~/.claude/skills` as the skill root. Spawn loads skill content via `LoadSkillWithDependencies()` and embeds it into SPAWN_CONTEXT at spawn time.
+
+**Source:** `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/skills/loader.go`, `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/orch/extraction.go`
+
+**Significance:** Deploying only to `~/.opencode/skill/` will not affect orch-go spawns. The only orch-go load path that matters is under `~/.claude/skills/**/SKILL.md`.
+
+---
+
+### Finding 6: OpenCode agent injection uses flat agent files, not SKILL.md
+
+**Evidence:** `skillc deploy --agent-dir` generates `~/.config/opencode/agent/{skill}.md` with frontmatter and the compiled body. `orchestrator.md` shows the deployed header and is timestamped with the latest compile.
+
+**Source:** `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/skillc/cmd/skillc/main.go` (deployAgentFile), `/Users/dylanconlin/.config/opencode/agent/orchestrator.md`
+
+**Significance:** For OpenCode sessions, the canonical orchestrator skill is the agent file, not the SKILL.md under `.opencode/skill/`.
+
+---
+
+### Finding 7: No hot reload for running sessions
+
+**Evidence:** Spawn loads skill content once via `LoadSkillWithDependencies()` and embeds it in SPAWN_CONTEXT. There is no re-read path in spawn for already-running sessions.
+
+**Source:** `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/orch/extraction.go`
+
+**Significance:** Deploying updated skills does not change any active session. The session must be restarted to pick up new skill content.
+
+---
+
+## Points of Confusion (Root Causes)
+
+1. **Deploy root ambiguity:** Running `skillc deploy` from inside a skill directory flattens output to `target/SKILL.md`, which looks like a valid deploy but bypasses the expected `meta/orchestrator` path.
+2. **Multiple skill roots:** Orchestrator can appear in `~/.claude/skills/`, `~/.opencode/skill/`, `~/.config/opencode/agent/`, and legacy `~/.claude/skills/src/**` locations. There is no cleanup step or warning about stale copies.
+3. **Hot reload assumptions:** Deploy updates only apply on new spawns; this isn't signaled to operators, leading to confusion when a running session doesn't reflect recent changes.
+
+---
+
+## Recommendation: What the Pipeline Should Look Like
+
+- **Single source of truth:** edit only `~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/**/.skillc/` files.
+- **Canonical deploy command:** `cd ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src && skillc deploy --target ~/.claude/skills --agent-dir ~/.config/opencode/agent`.
+- **Optional secondary deploy:** `skillc deploy --target ~/.opencode/skill` only if OpenCode skill scanning depends on it; otherwise deprecate.
+- **Cleanup step:** remove legacy directories (`~/.claude/skills/src/`, `~/.claude/skills/skills/src/`, `~/Documents/personal/orch-cli/skills/`) or mark them read-only with guardrails.
+- **Restart-required signal:** document in deploy output or operator checklist that active sessions must be restarted to pick up skill changes.
+
+---
+
+## Structured Uncertainty
+
+**What is tested:** deploy path mechanics (from code), current on-disk copies and timestamps, and orch-go skill load path.
+
+**What is inferred:** OpenCode's internal skill discovery behavior for `~/.opencode/skill/**/SKILL.md` (not tested here), but agent file generation is confirmed.
+
+---
+
+## References
+
+**Files Examined:**
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/.skillc/skill.yaml`
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/skillc/cmd/skillc/main.go`
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/skillc/pkg/compiler/compiler.go`
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/skills/loader.go`
+- `/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/orch/extraction.go`
+
+**Commands Run:**
+```bash
+pwd
+which skillc
+file /Users/dylanconlin/bin/skillc
+stat -f "%Sm %N" /Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/.opencode/skill/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/.opencode/skill/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/.claude/skills/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-cli/skills/orchestrator/SKILL.md \
+  /Users/dylanconlin/.config/opencode/agent/orchestrator.md
+```
+
+**Related Artifacts:**
+- `.kb/models/orchestrator-session-lifecycle/probes/2026-02-17-orchestrator-skill-injection-path-trace.md`
*** End Patch
